question: What are your uptime, performance, and support SLAs? What happens if you miss them?
title: Service Level Agreements: Uptime, Performance, Support, and Remedies
bullets: Define uptime SLA with calculation methodology, measurement window, and exclusions (e.g., scheduled maintenance)
Specify performance SLAs (latency, throughput, error rates) with measurable thresholds
Detail support SLAs (response/resolution times by severity, escalation paths)
Explain remedies for SLA breaches (service credits, termination rights, automatic vs. request-based application)
suggested_inputs: SLA document with definitions (outage, degradation, maintenance) and formulas
Public status page and 12–24 month incident/uptime history
Support policy with severity tiers, response/resolution SLAs, and escalation matrix
Service credit policy and examples of calculation/application

good.text: Uptime SLA: 99.9% monthly, measured as (total minutes − downtime)/total minutes, excluding scheduled maintenance with advance notice. Definitions for outage vs. degradation are documented. Historical uptime and incidents are published on a public status page.
Performance SLAs/SLOs include p95 latency targets, throughput limits, and error rate thresholds with dashboards for real-time monitoring. Degraded performance is explicitly defined and tracked.

Support tiers: Critical (≤1 hour response, 24x7), High (≤4 hours), Medium (≤1 business day), Low (≤2 business days). Clear escalation paths and on-call procedures are documented, including incident communication cadence.

SLA breaches trigger service credits based on severity/duration (e.g., tiered % credits). Credits are automatically applied or available upon request. Repeated breaches grant termination rights. Post-incident RCAs and corrective actions are provided.

partial.text: We offer a 99.9% uptime SLA, but definitions for downtime, degradation, and measurement methodology are not fully specified.
We monitor performance, but do not publish concrete latency/error SLOs or dashboards for customers.
Support response times are defined, but escalation paths, on-call coverage, or resolution targets are not clearly documented.
Service credits may be available for outages, but calculation methods and application (automatic vs. request) are unclear.

bad.text: We provide best-effort availability without formal SLAs or guarantees.
We offer SLAs but do not provide service credits or remedies if they are missed.
We guarantee 99.9% uptime without defining how it is measured or calculated.

follow_ups: Provide the last 12–24 months of uptime/incident history and a link to your public status page.
Define 'outage', 'degraded performance', and 'scheduled maintenance' and how each impacts SLA calculations.
Show how service credits are calculated and whether they are applied automatically.
What is your longest outage in the past two years? Provide RCA and corrective actions.
Do you provide real-time availability dashboards and incident communications?
